action.reasoning: Observation: the reproduced two-stage calibrated run achieved strong local val balanced accuracy (0.812) but hidden eval only 0.7903, while the historical best hidden eval remains 0.8092 from iteration 6. Analysis: this mismatch indicates validation is not reliably selecting the best hidden-test model, so further retraining based only on val may waste iterations. The most plausible explanation for losing the true best is checkpoint overwrite, not predict.py failure. Since stage1.pt and repro_stage1.pt exist, a cheap and potentially strong recovery is to recreate the exact stage-2 recipe from the original good stage1 checkpoint and preserve outputs under fresh names; this may recover the earlier hidden-test behavior better than using the reproduced stage1. Decision: run classifier-only stage 2 from checkpoints/stage1.pt with the known good calibrated class-balanced setup, save to a new immutable checkpoint, smoke-test locally, and submit that fresh checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.003 --weight_decay 0.05 --model_name convnext_tiny.fb_in22k_ft_in1k --img_size 224 --num_workers 4 --resume checkpoints/stage1.pt --freeze_backbone --weighted_sampler --loss cb_ce --beta 0.9999 --label_smoothing 0.0 --save_path checkpoints/recover_from_stage1_best.pt --calibrate && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/recover_from_stage1_best.pt --output pred_smoke.json --report_val

usage: train.py [-h] [--data_dir DATA_DIR] [--epochs EPOCHS]
                [--batch_size BATCH_SIZE] [--lr LR]
                [--weight_decay WEIGHT_DECAY] [--img_size IMG_SIZE]
                [--num_workers NUM_WORKERS] [--seed SEED]
                [--model_name MODEL_NAME] [--dropout DROPOUT]
                [--sampler {none,weighted}]
                [--loss {ce,logit_adjusted,cb_ce,ldam}]
                [--label_smoothing LABEL_SMOOTHING] [--logit_tau LOGIT_TAU]
                [--freeze_backbone] [--resume RESUME] [--save_path SAVE_PATH]
                [--calibrate]
